page: Vultr vcg-mi355x-252c-2872g-2304vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server designed for high-performance parallel computing and large-scale artificial intelligence workloads. The system is built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring an AMD processor configuration that provides 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs. This massive compute capacity is paired with 2872.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core to support memory-intensive applications.

The primary acceleration capability is delivered by eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family, providing a combined total of 2304 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. This GPU configuration is coupled with 14336 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, enabling rapid read and write operations for massive datasets. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server consolidates extreme compute, memory, and GPU resources into a single bare-metal instance. This design minimizes the network latency and synchronization overhead typically associated with multi-node clusters. The hardware profile is highly suited for workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, complex data science jobs, and high-throughput parallel processing.
description: Vultr vcg-mi355x-252c-2872g-2304vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server engineered for massive parallel computing and artificial intelligence workloads. The system is powered by an AMD x86_64 processor configuration delivering 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs, complemented by 2872.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight AMD MI355X CDNA4 GPUs, which provide a combined total of 2304 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage is supported by 14336 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, ensuring rapid data retrieval for large datasets. This server is optimized for resource-intensive tasks such as large language model training, deep learning, and high-performance data science jobs. By consolidating these resources into a single bare-metal instance, it offers qualitative cost efficiency by reducing multi-node clustering overhead.

tagline: An ultra-dense bare-metal accelerator platform featuring eight AMD CDNA4 GPUs and massive system memory for large-scale AI workloads.
bullet_points: Eight AMD MI355X CDNA4 GPUs delivering 2304 GB of total VRAM
252 AMD x86_64 physical cores and vCPUs for high-throughput parallel processing
2872.0 GB of system memory providing 11.4 GB per core
14336 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Optimized for large language model training and deep learning workloads
